title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis Authors: Angel Garcia de la Garza, Britton Sauerbrei, Jeff Goldsmith License: MIT. See LICENSE file details Version: 0.9

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: afpca implements Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis (aFPCA), method estimating directions variation functional data exhibit sharp changes smoothness. Standard FPCA methods impose global smoothness assumption can fail capture abrupt transitions underlying signal. afpca addresses combining fast scalable adaptive scatterplot smoothing technique probabilistic FPCA framework, allowing functional principal components smoothed adaptively. particularly useful applications neural recordings, sharp changes activity following stimulus must distinguished smooth baseline behavior.

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: can install development version afpca GitHub :

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: examples running adaptive FPCA. details use package can found XYZ. code uses function afpca::simulate_adaptive_functional_data() simulate 20 curves Yi(t)Y_i(t), =1,…,20i = 1,\dots,20 observed 200 time points common grid domain (0,1) gaussian noise. functions generated mean function two functional principal components varying temporal smoothness defined : μ(t)=t−3/2\mu(t) = t^{-3/2}sin⁡(π×t1/4)(t>1/2)\sin(\pi \times t^{1/4})(t>1/2) ϕk(t)=t−3/2\phi_k(t) = t^{-3/2}sin⁡(4π×k×t1/4)(t>1/2),k=1,2\sin(4\pi \times k \times t^{1/4})(t>1/2), k = 1,2 plot show simulated data looks like:  software performs adaptively-smoothed functional principal component analysis. main function package afpca:fpca.adapt. code illustrates : plots show estimated mean function functional principal components  plots shows two examples observed functions respective reconstructions.
code: library(afpca)  simulated_data <- simulate_adaptive_functional_data(N.subj = 20) #> Warning in rgl.init(initValue, onlyNULL): X11 error: GLXBadContext #> Warning: 'rgl.init' failed, will use the null device. #> See '?rgl.useNULL' for ways to avoid this warning. afpca.output <- fpca.adapt(data = simulated_data) #> Registered S3 method overwritten by 'lme4': #>   method           from #>   na.action.merMod car

what: Firing rates for 25 Neurons in the Motor Cortex — firing_rates_data
title: Firing rates for 25 Neurons in the Motor Cortex — firing_rates_data
text: dataset comes novel neuroscience research understand dynamics motor cortex activation involvement skilled movements. contains firing rates 25 neurons coming motor cortex mouse reaching food pellet. neuron-specific averages across 147 experimental trials.

Firing rates for 25 Neurons in the Motor Cortex — firing_rates_data
text: data frame 25 rows two columns including: neuron_no Integer, Neuron ID activation tidyfun tfd object containing firing rates neuron across 174 timepoints, spikes / ms

title: Firing rates for 25 Neurons in the Motor Cortex — firing_rates_data
text: Sauerbrei, Britton ., et al. "Cortical pattern generation dexterous movement input-driven." Nature 577.7790 (2020): 386-391. tidyfun manual: tidyfun.github.io/tidyfun

what: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: Decomposes functional observations using adaptive functional principal component analysis. method incorporates adaptive smoothness estimated decomposition using penalized likelihood framework

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
code: fpca.adapt(   data,   col_name = "name",   basis = "trunc.poly",   poly.degree = 2,   knots = NA,   nbs = 35,   n.comp = 18,   pve = 0.99,   normalize.scores = TRUE,   orthogonalize_scores = TRUE,   orthogonalize_fpcs = TRUE,   ntimes = 100 )

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: data data frame, matrix, tidyfun "tfd.reg" object functional observations. col_name Column name data. Required Y tibble. package expects column "dft" object tidyfun basis Type spline basis. "os" B-spline basis "trunc.poly" truncated polynomials (default) poly.degree degree spline basis knots Number knots used generate spline basis estimate mean function functional principal components nbs Number spline basis generate mean function functional principal components n.comp prespecified number principal components pve proportion variance explained; used choose number functional principal components normalize.scores normalize scores iteration (default true, recommended) orthogonalize_scores orthogonalize scores iteration (default true, recommended) orthogonalize_fpcs orthogonalize FPCs iteration (default true, recommended) ntimes Maximum number iterations

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: function returns object class "afpca" contains following: Y observed data. Pass either matrix, "dft" object tidyverse, tibble. tibble passed, "col_name" required Y_hat reconstructed fitted values mean vector estimated mean function fpcs tp (number timepoints) npc (number fpcs) matrix estimated eigenfunctions scores n (number subjects) npc (number fpcs) matrix estimated scores eigenvalues estimated eigenvalues .e. variances fpc scores Basis list containing penalized (Theta_beta) penalized (Theta_b) spline basis Theta concatenation (Theta_beta) penalized (Theta_b) spline basis coef estimated coefficients, coef %*% theta = fpcs convergence logical, algorithm converged .iter number iterations needed convergence

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
code: ## Using simulated data sim_data <- simulate_adaptive_functional_data(n.tp = 100) #> Warning: RGL: unable to open X11 display #> Warning: 'rgl.init' failed, will use the null device. #> See '?rgl.useNULL' for ways to avoid this warning. afpca_output <- fpca.adapt(data = sim_data, nbs = 10) #> Registered S3 method overwritten by 'lme4': #>   method           from #>   na.action.merMod car   ## Using firing rate data (uses tidyfun, install beforehand)  if (FALSE) {   data(firing_rates_data)   afpca_firing_output <- fpca.adapt(data = firing_rates_data, col_name = "activation", nbs = 10) }

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
